key_insight: A hybrid DFT + machine learning workflow can rapidly screen thousands of equimolar NaCl-type entropy-stabilized oxide compositions and identify the most promising stable candidates.
engineering_relevance: This provides a practical, scalable route for materials engineers to prioritize experimentally viable multicomponent oxides without running full first-principles calculations for every composition.
method: Built a reference database of binary and ternary ordered/disordered oxides using GGA and meta-GGA DFT.
Represented quinary equimolar candidates with SQS supercells and computed convex-hull distances and stabilization temperatures.
Trained supervised learning models, with an optimized multilayer perceptron giving the best predictions across 4368 compositions.
evidence_signal: The best model achieved about 4 kJ/mol test error while reducing explicit DFT calculations to roughly 10% of the quinary systems.
watch_item: Absolute stabilization temperatures may be biased by thermodynamic approximations, so predictions should be validated against experiment for borderline cases.
paper_title: Data-Driven Prediction of NaCl-Type Entropy-Stabilized Oxide Compositions from First-Principles and Supervised Learning
